evaluation.summary: Empirical measurements of the primorial gap variance ratio R(k) for k≥6 indicate a decreasing logarithmic trend, suggesting a possible scaling law that deviates from prior power‑law expectations.
next_directions: Collect additional R(k) measurements for higher k to improve statistical power and validate the observed trend.
Replicate the analysis with independent code and data pipelines to rule out hidden artifacts or boundary effects.
Develop theoretical models to explain the logarithmic decay and assess how it challenges existing primorial gap variance theories.
